items.description: applies_to.join() crashes when learning engine returns applies_to as a string instead of array
items.fix_applied: Added type guard: const appliesTo = Array.isArray(r.applies_to) ? r.applies_to.join(", ") : String(r.applies_to); — IMP-017 compliant

items.description: Revision catch block silently swallowed critical errors — restored old draft, logged one line, continued. Draft never changed, 3 rounds of reviews wasted on identical input.
items.fix_applied: Replaced silent catch with IMP-016 error escalation pattern: writes bug report with full context (round, markdown length, unresolved count, provider info), then throws BoardBlockedError. Board stops immediately instead of wasting LLM calls reviewing unchanged draft.

items.description: Learning engine returns applies_to as string because prompt didn't specify array format. Rulebook write didn't normalize.
items.fix_applied: TWO locations fixed: (1) Learning prompt now includes explicit JSON example with applies_to as array and IMPORTANT note that it MUST be a JSON array. (2) Rulebook write section now normalizes with: nr.applies_to = Array.isArray(nr.applies_to) ? nr.applies_to : [String(nr.applies_to)] — IMP-017 compliant

items.description: Split-verdict filter only excluded 'approved' reviewers. Conditional reviewers (minor issues only) were re-reviewed unnecessarily, wasting LLM calls and potentially generating conflicting feedback.
items.fix_applied: Added '&& reviewerStatus.get(key) !== "conditional"' to the filter predicate. Conditional reviewers are now correctly skipped in subsequent rounds. — IMP-003 compliant

items.description: pendingComplianceMap started as empty array []. Round 0 reviewer received no compliance data, reducing review quality.
items.fix_applied: Added generateInitialComplianceMap() function that performs keyword-based heuristic matching of rulebook rules against the initial draft markdown. Checks applies_to keywords and first 8 significant words of rule text against the markdown. Produces compliant (>=50% keyword coverage), non_compliant (>=20%), or not_applicable (<20%) for each rule. Round 0 reviewer now sees an initial compliance signal.

items.description: Rules ARB-016 through ARB-022 had applies_to as strings instead of arrays. This caused Bug 1 to crash the revision prompt.
items.fix_applied: Manually normalized all 7 rules: split comma-separated strings into proper JSON arrays. ARB-016: 5 elements, ARB-017: 5 elements, ARB-018: 4 elements, ARB-019: 1 element, ARB-020: 1 element, ARB-021: 1 element, ARB-022: 1 element.

new_issues.description: isSanityCheck parameter is passed to executeRound() but never used inside it. Dead parameter (IMP-006).
new_issues.fix_applied: FIXED: Removed isSanityCheck from executeRound() signature and the call site in executeBoard(). The local variable isSanityCheck still exists in executeBoard for the activeReviewers ternary but is no longer threaded as a parameter. tsc --noEmit passes clean.

new_issues.description: currentContract parameter in reviseRoleMarkdown() is declared but never referenced in the function body. Dead parameter (IMP-006).
new_issues.fix_applied: FIXED: Removed currentContract from reviseRoleMarkdown() signature (role-generator.ts) and the call site in board.ts. tsc --noEmit passes clean.

new_issues.description: priorRounds parameter in reviseRoleMarkdown() is declared but never referenced in the function body. Dead parameter (IMP-006).
new_issues.fix_applied: FIXED: Removed priorRounds from reviseRoleMarkdown() signature (role-generator.ts) and the call site in board.ts (the rounds.map(...) argument). tsc --noEmit passes clean.

new_issues.description: Module-level mutable state: runDir (line 134) and bugReportCounter (line 23) are shared across all calls. If executeBoard were ever called concurrently, these would conflict. Not immediately blocking since the tool runs single-threaded, but violates IMP-011 principle.
new_issues.fix_applied: FIXED: (1) Removed module-level 'let runDir' and 'let bugReportCounter'. (2) Created BoardContext interface { runDir, bugReportCounter } — instantiated fresh per executeBoard() call. (3) runDir is now a parameter of executeBoard(). (4) Removed setRunDir() export. (5) Updated index.ts: removed setRunDir import, passes runDir directly to executeBoard(). (6) writeBugReport, parseReviewerResponse, parseLeaderResponse all accept BoardContext. tsc --noEmit passes clean.

new_issues.description: group.findings.length used on LLM-returned conceptual_groups inner elements without type check. If LLM returns findings as a string, .length would return character count (wrong semantics) instead of finding count. Not blocking since only used for findingCount in fixChecklist display.
new_issues.fix_applied: FIXED: Changed to 'findingCount: Array.isArray(group.findings) ? group.findings.length : 0'. IMP-017 compliant. tsc --noEmit passes clean.

new_issues.description: Learning engine parse failure catch block (line 321) only logs error but doesn't escalate. The learning engine is less critical than revision (non-critical per IMP-016), so logging is acceptable, but the log doesn't include round number or context for post-mortem.
new_issues.fix_applied: FIXED: Changed to 'console.error(`[board] Learning engine failed (round ${round}, findings: ${fixChecklist.length}):`, ...)'. Now includes round number and finding count for post-mortem analysis. tsc --noEmit passes clean.

compliance_map.evidence: This fix map IS the self-check evidence. Every rule walked mechanically.
Bug 4 fix: conditional guard now covers all valid reviewer states (approved, conditional, reject, error, pending). Bug 5 fix: compliance map now covers round 0.
Bug 1 fix in role-generator.ts and Bug 3 fix in board.ts (two locations) cover all places where applies_to is consumed. Grep confirmed no other .join() on applies_to exists.
No new schemas created. generateInitialComplianceMap returns ComplianceEntry[] which is already defined and used.
NEW-1 (isSanityCheck), NEW-2 (currentContract), NEW-3 (priorRounds) dead parameters all removed from signatures and call sites. tsc --noEmit passes clean.
Bug 2 fix: revision catch block now writes bug report and throws BoardBlockedError instead of silently swallowing. All other catch blocks either log with context or throw.
No stubs created in this change.
This fix map is the closeout evidence. TypeScript compiles clean.
NEW-4 fixed: runDir and bugReportCounter moved from module-level into BoardContext, scoped per executeBoard() call. No shared mutable state remains.
No git operations in this change.
Postmortem deferred to completion of full implementation cycle.

Bug 2 fix: revision catch block now writes bug report with full context and throws BoardBlockedError. Critical operations (revision, parse) all escalate. Non-critical operations (learning engine) log with context.

Bug 1 fix: applies_to normalized before .join() in role-generator.ts. Bug 3 fix: applies_to normalized in learning prompt (explicit array format instruction) and in rulebook write (normalization guard). Bug 6 fix: existing corrupted data normalized in rulebook.json. New generateInitialComplianceMap also normalizes applies_to.

deep_analysis_summary: Analyzed all 856 lines of board.ts and 396 lines of role-generator.ts. Checked every .join()/.map()/.filter()/.slice() call against its input type. Checked every catch block against IMP-016 criticality. Checked every conditional guard against all terminal states (IMP-003). Found 6 additional issues (NEW-1 through NEW-6): 3 dead parameters (isSanityCheck in executeRound, currentContract and priorRounds in reviseRoleMarkdown), 1 module-level mutable state concern, 1 inner-element type safety gap on findings.length, and 1 learning engine catch without round context. All are minor severity. No other .join() calls on LLM-returned fields found. No other silent-swallow catch blocks found. The parseReviewerResponse and parseLeaderResponse functions already have proper error escalation with bug reports and BoardBlockedError. The reviewer verdict consumption in board.ts lines 670-675 and 697-702 properly guards all array fields with Array.isArray().
